title: Richly Spiced Syrian Lamb Kebabs
description: A pungent and flaky Syrian dish featuring ground annatto, served in a stone bowl, perfect for a midnight craving. This paleo-friendly recipe is made with wholesome ingredients and traditional spices.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the ground lamb, ground annatto, cumin, coriander, cayenne pepper, salt, and black pepper.
Mix well with your hands or a wooden spoon until just combined.
Stir in the chopped onion and minced garlic.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
Thread the lamb mixture onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Brush the skewers with olive oil and place them in a stone bowl or on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Bake the kebabs for 20-25 minutes, or until cooked through.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or cilantro if desired.
